questions.q: In which year did the Chauri Chaura incident take place?
questions.explanation: The Chauri Chaura incident occurred on 4 February 1922.

questions.q: What did the angry mob at Chauri Chaura set on fire?
questions.explanation: A crowd of protesters set fire to the police station at Chauri Chaura, killing those inside.

questions.q: Which national movement did Gandhi call off because of the Chauri Chaura violence?
questions.explanation: Distressed by the violence, Gandhi withdrew the Non-Cooperation Movement.

questions.q: Who was the leader that called off the movement after Chauri Chaura?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi decided to suspend the Non-Cooperation Movement following the incident.

questions.q: Chauri Chaura is located in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Chauri Chaura lies in the Gorakhpur district of present-day Uttar Pradesh, then part of the United Provinces.

questions.q: The principal reason Gandhi called off the Non-Cooperation Movement was that it had turned what?
questions.explanation: Gandhi opposed violence and halted the movement because protesters had killed policemen.

questions.q: The Chauri Chaura incident involved a confrontation between protesters and which group?
questions.explanation: The clash was between a procession of demonstrators and the local police.

questions.q: Chauri Chaura was, at the time of the incident, part of which colonial administrative province?
questions.explanation: Chauri Chaura was in the United Provinces of Agra and Oudh.

questions.q: What core principle of Gandhi's was violated by the events at Chauri Chaura?
questions.explanation: The killing of policemen contradicted Gandhi's foundational principle of non-violence, ahimsa.

questions.q: The Non-Cooperation Movement that ended after Chauri Chaura had been launched in which year?
questions.explanation: Gandhi launched the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920, two years before it was suspended.

questions.q: On what exact date did the Chauri Chaura incident occur?
questions.explanation: The clash and burning of the police station took place on 4 February 1922.

questions.q: Approximately how many policemen were killed in the burning of the Chauri Chaura police station?
questions.explanation: About 22 policemen died when the station was set ablaze and they were trapped inside.

questions.q: Chauri Chaura lies in which district of present-day Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Chauri Chaura is located in the Gorakhpur district.

questions.q: Shortly after Chauri Chaura, the Congress Working Committee met at which town to formally suspend mass civil disobedience?
questions.explanation: The Working Committee passed the Bardoli Resolution on 12 February 1922 suspending the movement.

questions.q: What triggered the confrontation between the police and the procession at Chauri Chaura?
options: A tax protest at a granary
Police firing on and provocation of a demonstration
The arrest of a Congress president
questions.explanation: The volunteers' procession was fired upon and provoked by the police, which enraged the crowd.

questions.q: In response to the incident and the suspension of the movement, many leaders were dismayed. Which prominent leader was in prison at the time and reportedly disagreed with the withdrawal?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru, then imprisoned, was among the leaders who felt the withdrawal was a setback.

questions.q: Following Chauri Chaura, Gandhi undertook what personal act of penance?
options: A vow of silence for a year
questions.explanation: Gandhi undertook a five-day penitential fast as atonement for the violence.

questions.q: Soon after Chauri Chaura, Gandhi himself was arrested and tried in March 1922 on charges of what?
questions.explanation: Gandhi was tried for sedition over his writings in Young India and sentenced to six years' imprisonment.

questions.q: The volunteers who marched at Chauri Chaura were largely protesting against which two grievances tied to the wider movement?
options: High food prices and liquor sales
The salt tax and indigo cultivation
questions.explanation: The demonstrators were protesting against high food prices and the sale of liquor, in line with Non-Cooperation aims.

questions.q: Which sense of timing made the suspension especially controversial among many nationalists?
options: It came before the movement had begun
It came after independence was promised
It came when the movement was at its peak momentum
questions.explanation: Many felt the movement was gaining great momentum, making the withdrawal a hard blow to morale.

questions.q: The Non-Cooperation Movement was closely allied with which contemporaneous Muslim-led agitation when Chauri Chaura occurred?
questions.explanation: Non-Cooperation ran in alliance with the Khilafat Movement, and both lost momentum after the suspension.

questions.q: Which historian wrote an influential study titled 'Event, Metaphor, Memory: Chauri Chaura 1922-1992'?
questions.explanation: Shahid Amin's 'Event, Metaphor, Memory' is a landmark microhistory of the Chauri Chaura incident.

questions.q: In the Chauri Chaura trials, how many of the accused were initially sentenced to death by the Sessions Court in 1923?
questions.explanation: The Sessions Court sentenced 172 of the accused to death, a number later sharply reduced on appeal.

questions.q: After the Allahabad High Court appeal in 1923, how many of the convicted men were ultimately hanged?
questions.explanation: The High Court confirmed death sentences for 19 of the accused, who were hanged in 1923.

questions.q: The accused in the Chauri Chaura case were defended in the appeal by which prominent Congress lawyer?
questions.explanation: Madan Mohan Malaviya led the legal defence at the appeal stage, helping reduce the number of executions.

questions.q: The Bardoli Resolution, passed in the wake of Chauri Chaura, was adopted by the Congress Working Committee on which date?
questions.explanation: The Working Committee passed the Bardoli Resolution on 12 February 1922, suspending mass civil disobedience.

questions.q: The volunteers involved at Chauri Chaura were locally organised under which type of nationalist body?
options: A district Swaraj Sabha and volunteer corps
questions.explanation: The marchers were part of local Congress-affiliated volunteer corps and Swaraj Sabha activity in the Gorakhpur area.

questions.q: A memorial and a martyrs' monument at Chauri Chaura commemorate the convicted volunteers; the station around which the violence centred was on which railway line?
questions.explanation: Chauri Chaura station lay on the Bengal and North Western Railway in the Gorakhpur region.

questions.q: The thana (police station) head officer whose actions provoked the crowd at Chauri Chaura held what rank?
questions.explanation: The local police station was under a daroga, the sub-inspector in charge of the thana, whose firing inflamed the crowd.

questions.q: In 2021 the Government of India launched a year-long centenary commemoration of which Chauri Chaura anniversary?
questions.explanation: The centenary (100th anniversary) celebrations of the 1922 incident were inaugurated in early 2021.
